agreements: Editable Demo means editing a defensive local Sandbox copy and must never mean modifying the source template.
The localStorage Sandbox is device-local user demonstration data, not a cloud trip, collaboration room, authorization identity, or myTrips entry.
Every persisted Sandbox record requires explicit schemaVersion and templateVersion fields.
Every Sandbox record and current snapshot must be treated as untrusted input and validated before rendering, saving, reset recovery, or Clone.
Expired, corrupted, stale, or incompatible persisted data must rebuild a safe local copy from the immutable template and must never trigger a Firebase write.
Reset must be explicit, confirmed, deterministic, and limited to the local Sandbox key without affecting myTrips, Offline Cache, or a real Firebase room.
The Clone converter must validate the current Sandbox snapshot and then apply the approved allowlist transformation rather than deep-copying Sandbox state.
Feature Introduction replay must not alter first-run eligibility, completion, seen, or dismissal records.
功能介紹 and 功能導覽 require distinct buttons, language, accessible names, test IDs, and flows because one is high-level app introduction and the other is a TripDetail contextual tour.
Demonstration expenses, tickets, settlements, and attachments may remain preview-only but must never create Firebase or Storage side effects.
The original Clone owner-only model, Demo-only CTA, disabled-by-default feature flag, Emulator-only technical development, no-production boundary, and separate Production Gate all remain mandatory.
disagreements: Reject any interpretation that editable Demo state permits mutation or persistence of the source template object.
Reject combining 功能介紹 and 功能導覽 into one action or one state machine.
Reject any implementation that uses Firebase, Storage, myTrips, or Offline Cache to persist Demo Sandbox edits.
Reject any Clone path that trusts or deep-copies the Sandbox without validator and allowlist boundaries.
unsupportedAssumptions: That data stored in localStorage is trusted merely because it was previously written by the application.
That the local-demo-sandbox identity can be handled as a real Firebase room ID.
That replaying FirstRunWelcomeDialog can reuse first-run completion side effects.
That editable itinerary and Checklist scope implicitly authorizes expense, ticket, attachment, settlement, collaboration, or Storage editing.
That the original six Assignments remain executable after this revised Amendment plan supersedes them.
missedRisks: Mutation leakage can occur through nested references unless every template read and Sandbox read returns a defensive copy.
A templateVersion upgrade can invalidate stored records and requires deterministic safe rebuild behavior.
Memory fallback can surprise users after refresh and therefore needs an explicit non-persistent status message.
Reset confirmation and Clone confirmation can be confused unless they remain visibly distinct actions with separate state.
Persisted Sandbox content can be manually tampered with and must not bypass place, Checklist, ID, member, or excluded-domain validation.
Preview-only expense, ticket, or attachment controls could accidentally expose write affordances even when the underlying persistence is disabled.
Feature Introduction replay could accidentally mark onboarding complete if event handlers are shared without a replay-specific contract.
questions: What exact schemaVersion and templateVersion compatibility policy will trigger migration rejection and safe reset?
How will memory-fallback status remain visible and accessible without blocking local Demo exploration?
Which explicit UI labels will distinguish reset, Clone, 功能介紹, and 功能導覽 on Desktop and Mobile?
Which tests prove zero Firebase, myTrips, Offline Cache, and Storage writes for every Demo edit and reset action?
How will the implementation prove that current Sandbox snapshot validation precedes Clone allowlist conversion?
revisedRecommendation: Adopt the human-confirmed editable Demo Amendment with strict defensive-copy and validation boundaries: derive a versioned persistent local Sandbox from an immutable template; support only itinerary and Checklist editing; provide confirmed local reset and a clearly disclosed memory fallback; keep all Demo actions outside Firebase, Storage, myTrips, and Offline Cache; validate the current Sandbox snapshot before the existing owner-only Clone allowlist converter; add separate Lobby and Settings Feature Introduction replay entries that never change onboarding state; preserve the existing trip-contextual Feature Tour as a distinct action; and retain every original feature-flag, Emulator-only, Production Gate, Rules, dependency, migration, and deploy restriction.

evidence.claim: The Round 1 amendment explicitly defines the human-confirmed editable Sandbox and Feature Introduction replay behavior.
The original approved Session and disabled Assignments remain the immutable safety and audit baseline.
The Demo factory remains the source-template boundary that must return fresh immutable source objects.
The high-level FirstRunWelcomeDialog and trip-contextual Settings Feature Tour are distinct existing surfaces.
The Settings menu contains the existing 功能導覽 action that must remain independent.
